chunks.text: Sarah Chasins | EECS at UC Berkeley Sarah Chasins: Faculty Home Page Larger Photo Sarah Chasins Assistant Professor Biography Sarah E. Chasins joined the EECS faculty in 2020. Her research focuses on programming languages and program synthesis, with an emphasis on work that brings together programming systems, HCI, and data. Much of her work is shaped by collaborations with scientists, social scientists, and other non-traditional programmers. Education 2019, Ph.D., Computer Science, University of California, Berkeley 2012, B.A., Computer Science, Psychology, Swarthmore College Research Areas Programming Systems (PS) Human-Computer Interaction (HCI) Research Centers EPIC Data lab (EPIC) Teaching Schedule CS 294-184. Building User-Centered Programming Tools , WeFr 09:00-10:29, Soda 405 Selected Honors and Awards Sloan Research Fellow , 2026 Google Faculty Research Award , 2021
